---
title: "About"
pagetitle: "About — Mahshad Sarikhani"
subtitle: "Physician, imaging researcher, builder of small useful tools"
toc: true
toc-location: right
toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.about-intro}
I am a physician based at Tehran University of Medical Sciences. My research lives at the boundary between abdominal radiology and machine learning, where I think there is more in medical data than we currently extract. An MRI holds information that gets lost in the report. Lab values, imaging, and clinical notes each tell a fragment of the story, and I want to build the tools that read them together.

The work focuses on **inflammatory bowel disease**, where damage hides, recurs, and accumulates over years. I build quantitative MRI pipelines, multimodal models, and scoring tools that make imaging structured enough to measure change and precise enough to act on. I collaborate with groups at UCL/Motilent in the UK and Mount Sinai in New York. If this kind of work matters to you, I would like to hear from you.
:::

## *Path*

::: {.timeline}

::: {.timeline-item}
[Jan 2024 — Present]{.timeline-date}
[Research Associate, Abdominal Imaging and AI]{.timeline-title}
[Department of Radiology, Shariati Hospital, Tehran University of Medical Sciences. Supervisor: Prof. Amir Reza Radmard. Leading quantitative MRE research in ulcerative colitis: GIQuant motility, bowel wall activity markers, chronicity features, and segment-level validation against endoscopy. Building reproducible pipelines that integrate imaging, structured radiology features, narrative reports, and clinical endpoints.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[Aug 2022 — Aug 2024]{.timeline-date}
[General Practitioner · Valiasr Local Medical Center]{.timeline-title}
[Part-time clinical work in Alborz, Iran during the transition into full-time research.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[Sep 2022 — Dec 2023]{.timeline-date}
[Research Assistant, Imaging and Clinical Data Science]{.timeline-title}
[Shahid Beheshti University of Medical Sciences. CT and MRI projects spanning protocol review, dataset construction, and statistical analysis. Contributed to PanCanAID-related pipelines including curated datasets and semi-automated CT segmentation workflows.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[May 2020 — May 2022]{.timeline-date}
[Medical Internship]{.timeline-title}
[Teaching hospitals of Shahid Beheshti University of Medical Sciences, Tehran. Two years of rotations across internal medicine, surgery, paediatrics, obstetrics, emergency, and outpatient clinics.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[2015 — 2022]{.timeline-date}
[Doctor of Medicine (MD)]{.timeline-title}
[Shahid Beheshti University of Medical Sciences, Tehran. Six-year MD program.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

:::

## International *collaborations*

::: {.timeline}

::: {.timeline-item}
[Ongoing]{.timeline-date}
[Motilent · University College London, UK]{.timeline-title}
[Cross-site coordination on feature definitions, QC, and analysis plans for motility MRI biomarkers. Supporting ECCO and ESGAR outputs and ongoing manuscript development.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[Ongoing]{.timeline-date}
[Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai, New York]{.timeline-title}
[Collaboration with Prof. Jean-Frédéric Colombel's group on IBD imaging research and manuscript development.]{.timeline-desc}
:::

:::

## Technical *skills*

::: {.skills-grid}

::: {.skill-category}
[Statistics & modelling]{.label}
[Multilevel Bayesian models, hierarchical regression, calibration, decision-curve analysis, leakage-aware validation for clustered clinical data.]{.items}
:::

::: {.skill-category}
[Programming & ML]{.label}
[Python, PyTorch, MONAI, scikit-learn, pandas, NumPy, Jupyter, Git.]{.items}
:::

::: {.skill-category}
[Medical AI & imaging]{.label}
[Deep learning for medical imaging, CLIP-style vision-language models, quantitative MRE feature extraction, DICOM preprocessing, image-text retrieval.]{.items}
:::

::: {.skill-category}
[LLMs & NLP]{.label}
[Structured information extraction from clinical text, agentic pipelines, tool-calling architectures, human-in-the-loop system design, prompt engineering.]{.items}
:::

::: {.skill-category}
[Research software]{.label}
[Streamlit prototypes for clinical research, modular repo structure, versioned releases with Zenodo DOIs, reproducible analysis workflows.]{.items}
:::

::: {.skill-category}
[Writing & workflow]{.label}
[LaTeX, Quarto, Obsidian + Zotero, Figma for workflow schematics.]{.items}
:::

:::

## Get in *touch*

::: {.contact-block}

The fastest way to reach me is by email. I read most of what people send me but reply slowly, especially during clinical weeks. If you are working on imaging in IBD, on multimodal medical models, or on any of the methods above, I would love to hear from you.

::: {.contact-links}
[mahshadsarikhani9696@gmail.com](mailto:mahshadsarikhani9696@gmail.com) · [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=iKhNW9AAAAAJ) · [GitHub](https://github.com/MahshadSa) · [ORCID](https://orcid.org/___)
:::

:::
